title: Add a decorrelated DART booster to the ensemble
summary: Current best solutions are all standard gbtree-style XGB/Cat variants and their blends, so ensemble diversity is limited.
rationale: Current best solutions are all standard gbtree-style XGB/Cat variants and their blends, so ensemble diversity is limited. XGBoost’s DART dropout booster is explicitly designed to reduce boosted-tree overfitting and often yields meaningfully different score geometry for rank blending.
implementation_hint: Train one XGBoost `booster=dart` model on the same validated feature set with conservative dropout settings; do not tune for best standalone CV only. Judge it by correlation with current OOF predictions and whether a 2- or 3-way rank/logit blend improves OOF and fold consistency.
expected_effect: Even if standalone CV is flat or slightly worse, it may improve the final blend through lower prediction correlation.
risk: DART is noisier and slower, and its early stopping can be unstable; easy to over-tune to OOF blend search.
